title: Treasury Securities - Federal Reserve Bank of New York

source.name: New York Fed Markets Data Hub

summary: This is an institutional reference page describing the New York Fed's Open Market Trading Desk role in Treasury securities operations for the SOMA portfolio. The page explains that the Desk buys and sells Treasuries in the secondary market and rolls over maturing holdings at auction, executing transactions with primary dealers. It also notes the NY Fed provides limited transaction services to approximately 200 central banks and official institutions. No discrete event, policy decision, or data release is contained in this source.
key_points: The NY Fed's Open Market Trading Desk is authorized by the FOMC to buy and sell Treasury securities for the System Open Market Account (SOMA)
The Desk purchases and sells Treasuries in the secondary market and rolls over maturing SOMA holdings at auction
Transactions are executed with primary dealers
Since end of 2008, the FOMC has directed the Desk to expand Treasury holdings in SOMA on several occasions to implement monetary policy
The NY Fed provides limited transaction services (purchases and sales of Treasuries) to about 200 central banks, governments, and official institutions
Recent announcements referenced include FedTrade Plus for SOMA Treasury operations (Mar 12, 2026) and Treasury Securities Small Value Exercise (Feb 23, 2026)
why_it_matters: This page provides foundational context for understanding how the Federal Reserve implements monetary policy through Treasury market operations. It is a structural reference document rather than a discrete market-moving event. Users seeking specific operational data should access the linked Treasury Operations data, SOMA Holdings, or Transaction Data resources rather than this overview page.
